mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates, directly supporting higher present values for future corporate cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which translates into positive short-term equity momentum as investors price in improved corporate earnings over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding two percent signals a severe demand shock that forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as markets reprice recession risk.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 drops >2%, or the signal fails to predict direction with >55% accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month unemployment rate increase above one and a half percentage points reflects acute labor market distress that suppresses consumer demand and triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 1.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 20-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 50% across multiple stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising real new home sales indicate accommodative monetary policy successfully transmitting to the housing sector, generating positive equity returns as financial and real economy sectors rally simultaneously.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based supply-side recovery that lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign during supply chain disruptions, or the combined signal predicts negative returns in >55% of quarters where both series rise.

mechanism_narrative: Surging housing starts during a post-crisis recovery phase signal renewed consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) outside the recovery regime, or housing starts fail to lead 5-day returns in >60% of recovery months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation during a period of near-zero policy rates triggers expectations of future monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently fail to predict negative returns, or the signal generates positive returns in >60% of observations during the specified regime.
